initial_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation:  tile the whole input for each
%  nonzero cell.  Zero cells give allzero blocks.
% -------------------------------------------------

%  Input dimensions
N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.   % number of rows of the input
M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.   % number of columns of the input

%  Output dimensions (each nonzero cell creates a square block of size NM,
%  the blocks are tiled in a grid of size (NN) rows  (MM) columns)
output_rows = N * N.
output_cols = M * M.

%  Output cells that belong to the output grid
output_cell(R, C) :- R >= 0, R < output_rows, C >= 0, C < output_cols.

%  Exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

%  Nonzero block: copies the whole input (so the colour at the subcell
%  (dr,dc) is the input colour at that subcell).  Any other colour is
%  forbidden.
:- input(i, j, V), V != 0,
   i >= 0, i < N,
   j >= 0, j < M,
   dr >= 0, dr < N,
   dc >= 0, dc < M,
   input(dr, dc, TileColor),
   R = i * output_rows + dr,
   C = j * output_cols + dc,
   :- output(R, C, Color), Color != TileColor.

%  Zero block: every subcell must be 0.
:- input(i, j, 0),
   i >= 0, i < N,
   j >= 0, j < M,
   dr >= 0, dr < N,
   dc >= 0, dc < M,
   R = i * output_rows + dr,
   C = j * output_cols + dc,
   :- output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

rounds.program_after:
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation:  tile the whole input for each
%  nonzero cell.  Zero cells give allzero blocks.
% -------------------------------------------------

%  Input dimensions
N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.   % number of rows of the input
M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.   % number of columns of the input

%  Output dimensions (each nonzero cell creates a square block of size NM,
%  the blocks are tiled in a grid of size (NN) rows  (MM) columns)
output_rows = N * N.
output_cols = M * M.

%  Output cells that belong to the output grid
output_cell(R, C) :- R >= 0, R < output_rows, C >= 0, C < output_cols.

%  Exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

%  Nonzero block: copies the whole input (so the colour at the subcell
%  (dr,dc) is the input colour at that subcell).  Any other colour is
%  forbidden.
:- input(i, j, V), V != 0,
   i >= 0, i < N,
   j >= 0, j < M,
   dr >= 0, dr < N,
   dc >= 0, dc < M,
   input(dr, dc, TileColor),
   R = i * output_rows + dr,
   C = j * output_cols + dc,
   :- output(R, C, Color), Color != TileColor.

%  Zero block: every subcell must be 0.
:- input(i, j, 0),
   i >= 0, i < N,
   j >= 0, j < M,
   dr >= 0, dr < N,
   dc >= 0, dc < M,
   R = i * output_rows + dr,
   C = j * output_cols + dc,
   :- output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

final_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Transformation:  tile the whole input for each
%  nonzero cell.  Zero cells give allzero blocks.
% -------------------------------------------------

%  Input dimensions
N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.   % number of rows of the input
M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.   % number of columns of the input

%  Output dimensions (each nonzero cell creates a square block of size NM,
%  the blocks are tiled in a grid of size (NN) rows  (MM) columns)
output_rows = N * N.
output_cols = M * M.

%  Output cells that belong to the output grid
output_cell(R, C) :- R >= 0, R < output_rows, C >= 0, C < output_cols.

%  Exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

%  Nonzero block: copies the whole input (so the colour at the subcell
%  (dr,dc) is the input colour at that subcell).  Any other colour is
%  forbidden.
:- input(i, j, V), V != 0,
   i >= 0, i < N,
   j >= 0, j < M,
   dr >= 0, dr < N,
   dc >= 0, dc < M,
   input(dr, dc, TileColor),
   R = i * output_rows + dr,
   C = j * output_cols + dc,
   :- output(R, C, Color), Color != TileColor.

%  Zero block: every subcell must be 0.
:- input(i, j, 0),
   i >= 0, i < N,
   j >= 0, j < M,
   dr >= 0, dr < N,
   dc >= 0, dc < M,
   R = i * output_rows + dr,
   C = j * output_cols + dc,
   :- output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.
